methodologyNote: Starbucks does not publish an official city-by-city store count anywhere in the world. The figures below are the best publicly reported third-party estimates (news investigations, retail-analytics scrapes of the store locator, and Starbucks' own regional storytelling). Different sources use different city boundary definitions, so totals for the same city can vary widely between reports — this is noted where it happens.

mostSaturatedCities.notes: Most Starbucks of any city in the world by this count.
Historically neck-and-neck with Seoul for the world's most Starbucks-saturated city.
Most Starbucks of any Chinese city. A separate, more recent estimate puts Shanghai closer to 600 stores — the spread illustrates how much city-level counts vary by source and date.
Long-running second-place U.S. city behind New York; also home to the world's largest Reserve Roastery.

flagshipRoasteries.notes: The very first Starbucks, opened March 30, 1971. Still open and among the most visited retail addresses in Seattle.
The first Starbucks Reserve Roastery, opened on Capitol Hill in December 2014; closed in September 2025 as part of the broader restructuring.
Was the largest Starbucks location in the world when it opened (30,000 sq ft).
Housed in the Palazzo Broggi, once Milan's stock exchange — a symbolic full-circle location given Howard Schultz's original inspiration came from Milanese espresso bars.

flagshipRoasteries.type: Reserve Roastery (Chelsea, plus a second location in the Empire State Building)

flagshipRoasteries.notes: At 43,000 sq ft across five stories, the largest Starbucks on Earth.
